title: blazewrit harness — Report step structured_output
description: Report = deliverable synthesis. The TERMINAL artifact of a NON-CODE flow (Review, Retro, Exploration, Spike, plan-standalone, Compound top-level summary). Synthesizes Investigate/Decide/Ground outputs into severity-tagged findings + action items, with an evidence trail. CODE flows (Feature/Bug/Refactor/Perf/Migration/Test/Chore/Release) have NO report step (README L60-62: failure_origin=report is invalid in code flows; Verify rejects). PRIMARY DISCRIMINANT is `result` (synthesized | empty_clean | escalate) — the terminal-artifact branch model (P1/principle 1/principle 3); report_type sub-discriminates the success/empty branches. TARGET RUNTIME: grammar-constrained generation — M1 enforces SHAPE/enum/required/oneOf-branch at token-generation time; the model literally cannot emit a wrong shape. Fields needing TRUTH (evidence re-read, verify_probe re-execution, chain match, same-file parallel mutual-exclusion, count↔stdout derivation) are closed by SIBLING deterministic code checks listed in `x-m2-validator-contracts`, NOT by any keyword here. STORAGE §5: this output persists to Postgres step_runs jsonb; legacy '.blazewrit/reports/{id}.md' / '.blazewrit/plans/{id}-plan.md' paths are PRE-§5 and are DROPPED — there is no artifact_path field; cross-step refs are RowRefs.
$comment: result is the TOP-LEVEL discriminant (synthesized | empty_clean | escalate). The outer oneOf branches on result: synthesized/empty_clean carry the success/empty terminal-artifact shape (report_type + body + findings + action_items + based_on + declared_next_step, with a nested report_type oneOf for per-flow obligations); escalate carries only escalate_details and is emittable WITHOUT a valid report_type (so a code-flow / missing-flow_type precondition violation is representable — README report_type assignment rule). based_on, declared_next_step, findings[].evidence_ref, findings[].verify_probe carry M2 validator contracts (truth, not shape).

oneOf.$comment: result ∈ {synthesized, empty_clean} — the SUCCESS / legitimately-empty terminal artifact. report_type + the common synthesis fields are required; the nested report_type oneOf tightens per-flow obligations. empty_clean additionally requires empty_details; synthesized requires body.summary present (enforced by body.required=[summary]).
oneOf.title: result=synthesized|empty_clean

result.description: synthesized = sound deliverable synthesized (normal success). empty_clean = legitimately nothing to report (principle 3: empty-as-verdict, NOT error) — a report_type-specific minimal artifact + empty_details.

report_type.description: DISCRIMINANT (README report_type assignment rule). Which non-code flow this report terminates — mechanically mapped 1:1 from the orchestrator-injected flow_type (review/retro/exploration/spike/plan-standalone/compound). Drives the per-branch required obligations in the nested oneOf. plan_standalone is the snake_case slug of flow-stem 'plan-standalone'.

body.description: Deliverable body slot (README 'body 생산 규칙'). The SOLE narrative slot for content/summary/design+next-step. summary is required+non-empty ONLY when result=synthesized (gated by the outer-branch if/then below); on result=empty_clean summary may be absent/empty — empty_clean is EXEMPT from the per-report_type minimum-content bar (that bar gates synthesized), so an exploration-empty case carries empty_details without a non-empty body.summary. sections carries optional structured body (plan_standalone design document). next_step is required for plan_standalone (minimum-bar 'next step').

summary.description: Top-level narrative synthesis: what was investigated/reviewed and the key conclusion (README Activity 2 Synthesize). Required+non-empty for every report_type on the synthesized branch (the deliverable's lede). exploration minimum-bar 'content 존재' = this non-empty.

sections.description: Optional structured body (plan_standalone design document, etc). Required for plan_standalone (design-document minimum-bar) — Decide(Design) 산출을 narrative로 옮긴 것이지 새 설계가 아니다.

next_step.description: Next step narrative — REQUIRED for report_type=plan_standalone (minimum-bar 'next step 명시'). Optional otherwise.

findings.description: Severity-tagged findings synthesized from Investigate/Review/Retro (README 'findings: [id, statement, severity, evidence_ref]'; agent.md L22-26 adds impact + verify_probe). Each finding carries: evidence_ref (SHAPE present here; 'claim verified'/evidence-traceable is the IRREDUCIBLE floor + M2 trace), source_tool provenance (R26 floor, REQUIRED), verify_probe (R20, re-executed by M2), and an unverified flag (R13 KEEP polarity — NOT forbidden). Empty array allowed (e.g. a clean review with no issues → result=empty_clean), but the reviewer spot-checks 2-3 against source.

action_items.description: Concrete next steps (README 'action_items: [description, priority, owner?, followup_flow_ref?]'). priority is re-projected from the origin finding/risk severity (Report does NOT invent a new priority — 'priority 생산 규칙'). Per-flow floor: only retro requires >=1 action item (reviewer 'retro=≥1 action item'); review/exploration/spike/plan_standalone/compound do not floor action_items (review-with-0-findings → empty_clean; spike satisfied by feasibility_verdict; compound satisfied by body.summary + sub_flow_summaries). That floor is encoded in the nested report_type oneOf. followup_flow_ref links a queued follow-up flow ROW (§5 RowRef, never a path).

feasibility_verdict.$comment: Required only on the spike branch (nested oneOf). Field name unified to feasibility_verdict (cross-cutting decision 3); enum lowercase go|no_go|conditional.

task_list.$comment: Required only on the plan_standalone branch (nested oneOf). R19 machine-consumable task list carried verbatim/refined from Decide — co-exists with body.sections+body.next_step (the human-readable narrative); neither drops the other (cross-cutting decision 5).

sub_flow_summaries.$comment: Required only on the compound branch (nested oneOf). Machine-consumable sub-flow aggregation. Coexists with body.summary (the human-readable top-level summary).

empty_details.$comment: Required only when result=empty_clean (enforced by the outer-branch allOf/if-then below).

based_on.description: §5 upstream RowRefs the report synthesized from. README 'based_on: { investigate_ref, decide_ref, ground_ref }'. EVERY value is a Postgres step_runs row id, NEVER a file path (legacy '.blazewrit/grounds/**' is PRE-§5). All three keys required here because Report's inputs (README Inputs) are Investigate + Decide + Ground outputs; a report not grounded in those three rows is unsourced. Report's based_on carries ONLY its declared input keys — investigate_ref/decide_ref/ground_ref (+ the Compound sub_flow_refs) — so it is INLINED here rather than allOf-ing the shared BasedOn $def (which also declares triage_ref/spec_ref/test_ref/report_ref, none of which are Report inputs); additionalProperties:false then rejects any non-input key. sub_flow_refs is the additional Compound provenance key (array of RowRefs resolving the aggregated sub-flow rows, distinct from the input-side sub_flow_id) coexisting with sub_flow_summaries. x-m2-validator: each ref must resolve to an existing step_run row of the expected step in this flow.

sub_flow_refs.$comment: §5 — Compound source provenance. Coexists with the richer sub_flow_summaries object (cross-cutting decision 5: carry both the machine shape and the provenance refs).
sub_flow_refs.description: Array of sub-flow RowRefs aggregated by a Compound report (README Compound input shape). Present for report_type=compound; each is a Postgres flow/step_run row id, never a path.

declared_next_step.$comment: R16 — producer DECLARES but does not free-fill the authoritative next step; orchestrator injects expected_next_step; chain match is M2.
declared_next_step.description: R16 (agent.md L28/L31-33; reviewer criterion 6 CRITICAL). REQUIRED on this synthesized|empty_clean branch only — the escalate branch carries no declared_next_step (orchestrator routes via NEEDS_CONTEXT, not the normal successor). Report's declared successor — for a terminal non-code flow this is typically 'verify' (plan-standalone: Decide→Report→Verify). ADVISORY: the schema carries the declared value; the authoritative value is orchestrator-injected. x-m2-validator (synthesized|empty_clean only): declared_next_step == orchestrator's expected_next_step (chain-divergence check). Mismatch = reviewer FAIL 'R16 chain violation'.

allOf.$comment: synthesized ⇒ body.summary present+non-empty (the deliverable's lede + per-report_type minimum-content bar). empty_clean is EXEMPT — it may carry an empty/absent body.summary alongside empty_details (README L187 exploration-empty path). summary.minLength:1 on the property handles non-emptiness; this if/then handles presence only on the synthesized branch.

SYNTHESIZED-ONLY per-report_type minimum-content obligations. These gate result=synthesized ONLY (README L183); empty_clean is EXEMPT (satisfied by empty_details alone). review ⇒ >=1 finding; retro ⇒ >=1 action_item; spike ⇒ feasibility_verdict; plan_standalone ⇒ task_list + body.sections + body.next_step; compound ⇒ sub_flow_summaries.

review synthesized ⇒ >=1 finding. A 0-findings clean review is a legitimately-empty verdict and routes to empty_clean (README L198), NOT synthesized; so a synthesized review must carry at least one finding (no degenerate summary-only synthesized review).
retro synthesized ⇒ >=1 action item (the sole action-item floor).
spike synthesized ⇒ feasibility_verdict (go/no_go/conditional). A spike with no verdict ESCALATES (README L190), so it is never empty_clean.
plan_standalone synthesized ⇒ task_list (machine) + body.sections + body.next_step (human-readable); both shapes coexist (cross-cutting decision 5).

compound synthesized ⇒ sub_flow_summaries (machine aggregation) AND based_on.sub_flow_refs (provenance); both coexist with body.summary (cross-cutting decision 5). README L125/L192/reviewer L253: sub_flow_refs is REQUIRED for a compound synthesized report.

VERDICT-OR-ESCALATE report_types have NO empty_clean case (README L190 spike, L191 plan_standalone). spike: verdict-or-escalate (verdict ⇒ synthesized, no verdict ⇒ escalate). plan_standalone: Decide(Design) present ⇒ synthesized, absent ⇒ escalate (failure_origin=decide) — there is no legitimately-empty plan. So report_type ∈ {spike, plan_standalone} ⇒ result must be synthesized (this outer branch already excludes escalate; this forbids empty_clean). Machine-enforces the prose unreachable-state rule.

oneOf.$comment: REVIEW — Pass1 critical / Pass2 informational (agent.md L39). Findings-driven. No action-item floor: a clean review with 0 findings routes to empty_clean.
oneOf.title: report_type=review

oneOf.$comment: RETRO — process observations. The >=1 action-item floor is gated to result=synthesized (see the synthesized-only allOf below); empty_clean retro is EXEMPT (it is satisfied by empty_details). README L188: a retro whose upstream (Decide/Investigate) is WELL-FORMED but yields 0 learnings is a legitimate empty_clean; only a MISSING/MALFORMED upstream escalates, with failure_origin = the broken input's origin (Investigate-missing ⇒ investigate, Decide-missing ⇒ decide; per the Input-preconditions table — NOT hardcoded to decide).
oneOf.title: report_type=retro

oneOf.$comment: EXPLORATION — 'content over form' (agent.md L41). Minimum-bar = body.summary non-empty (gated to synthesized via the outer body.summary if/then); action items NOT required.
oneOf.title: report_type=exploration

oneOf.$comment: SPIKE — feasibility assessment; MUST end with a go/no_go/conditional verdict (reviewer Spike criterion). spike has NO empty_clean case (verdict-or-escalate, README L190): result is forced to synthesized by the verdict-or-escalate allOf above, so feasibility_verdict always applies here.
oneOf.title: report_type=spike

oneOf.$comment: PLAN_STANDALONE — Decide(Design)→Report→Verify terminal Design doc. On result=synthesized carries BOTH the machine-consumable task_list (R19, verbatim/refined from Decide, deferral forbidden — reviewer criterion 9) AND the human-readable body.sections (design document) + body.next_step (cross-cutting decision 5). plan_standalone has NO empty_clean case (design-or-escalate, README L191): result is forced to synthesized by the verdict-or-escalate allOf above, so the task_list/body.sections/body.next_step minimum-bar always applies here.
oneOf.title: report_type=plan_standalone

oneOf.$comment: COMPOUND — top-level summary aggregating sub-flow results (README L7). On result=synthesized carries BOTH the machine-consumable sub_flow_summaries AND body.summary + based_on.sub_flow_refs provenance. sub_flow_summaries is required only when result=synthesized (see the synthesized-only allOf below); empty_clean is EXEMPT (README L183). No action-item floor.
oneOf.title: report_type=compound

oneOf.$comment: result=escalate — Report cannot synthesize a sound deliverable within its own scope (principle 1: synthesis itself is blocked, not an auxiliary tool). Emittable WITHOUT a valid report_type (a code-flow / missing-or-malformed flow_type precondition violation → escalate, failure_origin=upstream — README report_type assignment rule + Constraint). escalate_details is required; based_on is OPTIONAL provenance of whichever upstream rows WERE inspected before escalating (so a partial-input escalate — e.g. spike with prototype missing but Investigate/Decide present, failure_origin=implement; or a self-cap escalate where all inputs were well-formed, failure_origin=self — is machine-traceable, not only a prose string). Orchestrator routes via the existing NEEDS_CONTEXT path; Report does NOT diagnose or queue followups.
oneOf.title: result=escalate

based_on.$comment: OPTIONAL on escalate (unlike the synthesized|empty_clean branch where investigate/decide/ground are required). At escalate time the inputs may be PARTIAL or absent, so no key is required here — Report records only the upstream rows it actually consumed before escalating, making escalate provenance machine-resolvable for the orchestrator's NEEDS_CONTEXT routing + 5-accumulated-fail cap. All values are §5 RowRefs.
based_on.description: §5 upstream RowRefs the report inspected before escalating (optional, partial). Lets the orchestrator trace WHICH upstream rows were consumed for a partial-input or self-cap escalate. Each value is a Postgres step_runs row id, never a path. INLINED to the same Report-input key set as the synthesized branch (investigate_ref/decide_ref/ground_ref/sub_flow_refs) — all OPTIONAL here — rather than allOf-ing the shared BasedOn $def, so additionalProperties:false rejects any non-input key. x-m2-validator: any present ref resolves to an existing row of the expected step in this flow.

sub_flow_refs.$comment: §5 — optional Compound source provenance when a compound report escalates after partially inspecting sub-flow rows.
sub_flow_refs.description: Array of sub-flow RowRefs inspected before escalating (compound). Each is a Postgres flow/step_run row id, never a path.

CrossCheckedCount.$comment: NOT a CountClaim: task_count/sub_flow_count count things the model SYNTHESIZED (the plan's task array, the assembled sub-flow array), not the stdout of a measuring command. Forcing CountClaim here would force fabricating a command+sha for a non-tool-measured quantity (the exact failure CountClaim was built to PREVENT). So this is a bare integer whose truth is the x-m2 array-length cross-check (value == len of the sibling array), not a stdout re-hash.
CrossCheckedCount.description: A count cross-checked against a sibling array length (task_count == len(task_list.tasks); sub_flow_count == len(sub_flow_summaries.sub_flows) == len(based_on.sub_flow_refs)). x-m2-validator: assert the integer equals the cardinality of the array it counts — NOT a CountClaim (no command/stdout backs a model-synthesized array).

Finding.description: One severity-tagged finding (README 'findings: [id, statement, severity, evidence_ref]' + agent.md L22-26 impact/verify_probe). evidence_ref present = SHAPE; evidence-traceable / 'claim verified' = irreducible floor closed by M2 (re-read the cited source) + the unverified KEEP-polarity flag. source_tool is the REQUIRED R26 provenance floor on every finding item. severity uses the SINGLE shared risk Severity scale low|med|high|critical (cross-cutting decision 2 / principle 6 — no second scale invented).

id.description: Stable finding id (e.g. 'F1') for action_item/cross-reference linkage.

statement.description: The finding itself — what was observed/concluded.

severity.$comment: SINGLE risk scale (cross-cutting decision 2 / principle 6). Reuses the shared Severity $def low|med|high|critical; the compatibility-issue scale (fatal|high|medium|low) is NOT cited here.

evidence_ref.$comment: SHAPE present here; 'evidence_ref 추적 가능' / 'claim verified' is the irreducible/M2 trace, NOT closeable by grammar.
evidence_ref.description: Evidence backing the finding (README 'evidence_ref'; agent.md L24 'file:line reference, data, or reproduction steps'). Either an upstream RowRef (a Ground fact row / Investigate finding row in Postgres) OR an inline read-with-provenance record (file:line the agent read directly) OR reproduction steps. Presence is grammar-enforced; that the evidence ACTUALLY supports the claim and the cited lines exist is the M2 trace + irreducible floor.

oneOf.$comment: Upstream evidence: points at a Postgres row (Ground fact / Investigate finding). §5 RowRef, never a path.
Direct evidence: a file:line the agent read. Uses the SourceManifest $def so M2 can re-read + assert every cited line exists (R36). A read INPUT path is allowed (distinct from §5 output-as-file ban).
Reproduction-steps evidence (agent.md L24 'reproduction steps') when neither a row nor a static file:line — a runnable sequence. Truth = the verify_probe sibling re-execution.

impact.description: Consequence if the finding is ignored (agent.md L25 'Impact'). Optional narrative.

verify_probe.$comment: R20 — re-runnable assertion. Reviewer hard-FAILs on a missing verify_probe; re-executes random probes. Truth is the re-execution, not the shape.
verify_probe.description: R20 mechanical probe validating this finding (agent.md L26 'mechanical command/file_exists/grep'; reviewer '모든 finding에 verify_probe 명시. 누락 시 FAIL'). SHAPE here; x-m2-validator RE-EXECUTES command and compares to expected_result.

source_tool.$comment: R26 provenance floor — REQUIRED on every finding item.
source_tool.description: The tool/origin that produced this finding (re-read source, ed query, grep, upstream step). Irreducible provenance floor — a finding without source_tool is unsourced and rejected.

unverified.$comment: R13 KEEP polarity — DESIGNED to persist; Verify is the single gate. NO 'no unverified remain' forbid (legacy agent.md L57 / reviewer criterion 3 deleted by the cleanup, per enforcement.md L41 + UnverifiedFlag $def).
unverified.description: R13 flag: true = this finding (e.g. a training-data-based claim, agent.md L47) could not be verified by Report itself and MUST propagate to Verify. POLARITY: never forbidden at this step. Grammar enforces presence; whether unverified:false is honest is the irreducible self-asserted-truth floor (mitigated, not eliminated).

ActionItem.description: Concrete next step (README 'action_items: [description, priority, owner?, followup_flow_ref?]'; agent.md L27 'specific: "Refactor src/auth/token.ts:45-78..."' not vague). priority uses the SINGLE shared risk Severity scale low|med|high|critical, re-projected from the origin finding/risk severity (Report does NOT invent priority). followup_flow_ref links a queued follow-up flow ROW (§5).

description.description: Specific imperative action (agent.md L48 — concrete file:line target, not 'improve auth').

priority.$comment: SINGLE shared risk scale (cross-cutting decision 2). Reuses the shared Severity $def low|med|high|critical — re-projected from the origin finding/risk severity, NOT an invented scale. priority token is 'med', not 'medium'.

followup_flow_ref.$comment: §5 — a queued follow-up FLOW row id (Decide.followup_flows linkage, README Activity 3). RowRef, NEVER a path. x-m2-validator: must resolve to an existing flow/work_item row.
followup_flow_ref.description: Optional RowRef to the follow-up flow this action item queues (README 'followup_flow_ref?'). Realizes README Activity 3 'followup_flows 추출 (Decide.followup_flows 연결)'. A Postgres row id, not a file path.

FeasibilityVerdict.description: Spike feasibility verdict (README 'feasibility_verdict'; reviewer Spike criterion). Field name unified to feasibility_verdict and enum lowercase go|no_go|conditional (cross-cutting decision 3). Required on the spike branch only. CONDITIONAL carries conditions[]. rationale + evidence_ref are required (the prototype-observation basis the production rule mandates).

rationale.description: prototype 관찰 → verdict 근거 (required). Why this GO/NO_GO/CONDITIONAL.

conditions.description: Conditions that must hold — REQUIRED+non-empty when verdict=conditional (충족돼야 할 조건). Omitted otherwise. minItems:1 so a conditional verdict cannot vacuously pass with an empty conditions list.

evidence_ref.$comment: §5 RowRef to the Implement(prototype) result row — the prototype basis of the verdict. x-m2-validator: resolves to the prototype Implement row.
evidence_ref.description: RowRef to the Implement(prototype) result row backing the verdict (required — verdict의 prototype 근거).

EmptyDetails.description: result=empty_clean payload (principle 3: legitimately-empty verdict, NOT error — clean review / empty exploration). reason + evidence required; evidence points at the fact that 'clean' is real, not a missing/malformed input. suggested_action is optional (e.g. exploration reframe).

reason.description: 왜 보고할 내용이 합법적으로 없는가 (e.g. 'clean: no issues found').

evidence.$comment: Points at the Ground/Investigate fact that makes 'clean' a fact, not a deficit. Either a §5 RowRef or a read-with-provenance manifest.
evidence.description: Evidence that 'clean' is a fact (a Ground/Investigate fact ref), not a missing input. RowRef or source_manifest.

suggested_action.description: Optional follow-up hint (exploration 등). none = no follow-up; reframe_request = suggest re-framing the request.

EscalateDetails.description: result=escalate payload (principle 1: synthesis itself blocked). reason + failure_origin + evidence. Report does NOT diagnose — failure_origin names the nearest origin the input precondition points at. failure_origin is a REPORT-LOCAL enum [upstream, investigate, decide, implement, self] — 'upstream' is NOT in the shared FailureOrigin $def (which is Verify's routing enum), so it is defined locally here (cross-cutting decision 4 / the report mismatch resolution). 'self' = Report's OWN active-depth wall_s/token cap exhausted mid-synthesis (whichever depth cap was active — Shallow wall_s=30/tokens=5k OR Deep wall_s=180/tokens=15k; self-budget exhaustion — distinct from the shared FailureOrigin 'cap_exceeded' which is Verify's flow-level cap); the prior input preconditions were all met, so no upstream origin applies.

failure_origin.$comment: Report-local enum, NOT the shared FailureOrigin $def — 'upstream' and 'self' are values the shared enum lacks. upstream = generic/missing upstream; investigate/decide/implement = the concrete producer the precondition points at; self = Report's own active depth-cap (wall_s/token) exhaustion mid-synthesis — Shallow (wall_s=30/tokens=5k) or Deep (wall_s=180/tokens=15k), whichever was active.
failure_origin.description: Nearest origin of the synthesis-blocking fault (Report does not deeply diagnose). upstream = generic missing/malformed upstream (e.g. code-flow / missing flow_type); investigate = findings source missing; decide = decision/design source missing; implement = prototype result missing (spike); self = Report's OWN active depth cap exhausted before synthesis completed — whichever cap was active (Shallow wall_s=30/tokens=5k OR Deep wall_s=180/tokens=15k) — inputs were well-formed but the deliverable could not be finished within budget (Step Depth Policy self-cap branch).

evidence.description: What is missing/malformed (a ref or description of the missing/malformed field).

Task.$comment: R19/R33 task — carried VERBATIM/refined from Decide.task_list for plan-standalone. parallel_marker has an M2 same-file mutual-exclusion contract; task_count is a CrossCheckedCount (== len(tasks)), NOT a CountClaim.
Task.description: One R19 task in a plan-standalone Design synthesis (agent.md L29 'Decide의 task_list verbatim 또는 정제. Spec/Implement이 직접 consume — deferral 금지'; decide.md L58-66 task shape). Terminal: this report IS the consumable plan. deferral ('Spec이 finalize') is a reviewer FAIL (criterion 9). target_files drives the M2 same-file parallel-exclusion check.

description.description: Imperative concrete action (decide.md L60). No deferral language.

inputs.description: Upstream artifact RowRefs this task consumes (decide.md L61 'inputs: [<artifact ref>]'). §5 RowRefs, not paths.

target_files.$comment: File paths the task WRITES — these are the keys the M2 same-file parallel-exclusion check compares across tasks. A WRITE-target path is a plan output target, distinct from §5 storage-of-workflow-output ban.
target_files.description: Files this task will create/modify (decide.md L62 'outputs'). x-m2-validator (with parallel_marker): two tasks sharing any target file MUST both have parallel_marker=false.

depends_on.description: Task ids this depends on (decide.md L63).

acceptance_test.description: How task completion is judged (decide.md L64 '{ type, target, expected }').

verify_probe.description: R20 mechanical probe for this task (decide.md L65, L76 '모든 task에 mechanical execute 가능한 probe').

parallel_marker.$comment: R19/R33 — same-file tasks => parallel_marker=false is an M2 cross-task check, not a keyword.
parallel_marker.description: Whether this task may run in parallel with siblings. x-m2-validator (REQUIRED): tasks sharing a target_file path MUST both be false. Grammar cannot see cross-task overlap.

TaskList.$comment: plan-standalone Design synthesis (R19). task_count is a CrossCheckedCount (== len(tasks)), NOT a CountClaim — the task array is model-synthesized, not a measuring command's stdout. Coexists with body.sections+body.next_step (cross-cutting decision 5).
TaskList.description: R19 task list for plan-standalone (agent.md L29; decide.md L58). Carried verbatim/refined from Decide; this report is the terminal consumable plan. The MACHINE-consumable half of the plan_standalone deliverable; the human-readable design document lives in body.sections + body.next_step. task_count is a CrossCheckedCount cross-checked against len(tasks) — NOT a CountClaim, since no command measures a synthesized task list.

task_count.$comment: NOT a CountClaim — the task array is model-synthesized (carried/refined from Decide), not the stdout of a measuring command. x-m2-validator: task_count == len(tasks) (array-length cross-check), not a stdout re-hash.
task_count.description: Number of tasks in this plan. Cross-checked count == len(task_list.tasks); never a CountClaim (no command measures a synthesized task list).

SubFlowSummaries.$comment: Compound top-level aggregation. sub_flow_count is a CrossCheckedCount (== len(sub_flows) == len(based_on.sub_flow_refs)), NOT a CountClaim — the sub_flows array is model-assembled, not a measuring command's stdout. Coexists with based_on.sub_flow_refs + body.summary (cross-cutting decision 5).
SubFlowSummaries.description: Compound flow top-level summary (README L7). Aggregates each sub-flow's terminal result row. The MACHINE-consumable half of the compound deliverable; body.summary is the human-readable top-level summary and based_on.sub_flow_refs the provenance ref list. sub_flow_count is a CrossCheckedCount cross-checked against len(sub_flows) and len(based_on.sub_flow_refs) — NOT a CountClaim, since no command measures a model-assembled sub-flow set. Each entry references the sub-flow's row (§5 RowRef) and records its terminal_result.

flow_ref.$comment: §5 — the sub-flow's Postgres flow/step_run row id, never a path.
flow_ref.description: RowRef to the sub-flow's terminal row whose result is being summarized.

outcome.$comment: Report-SYNTHESIZED, not carried verbatim from the Compound input shape (which has no 'outcome' field). Derived per README 'outcome 생산 규칙' from this sub-flow's verdict_ref/finding_refs/terminal_result.
outcome.description: One-line outcome of the sub-flow, SYNTHESIZED by Report (README 'outcome 생산 규칙') from the sub-flow's verdict_ref (if any) + finding_refs + terminal_result — not an upstream-carried field. If a sub-flow carries NEITHER verdict_ref NOR finding_refs (nothing to summarize beyond terminal_result), outcome degrades to the terminal_result restatement (e.g. 'abandoned, no findings'); a sub-flow whose terminal_result itself is missing/malformed is an input-precondition fault → result=escalate, failure_origin=upstream (not a synthesizable outcome).

terminal_result.description: Sub-flow terminal classification (README Compound input shape 'terminal_result: completed | abandoned | suspended').

verdict_ref.$comment: §5 — present when the sub-flow produced its own Report (e.g. a Spike sub-flow's go/no_go).
verdict_ref.description: Optional RowRef to the sub-flow's own report row (Spike GO/NO_GO etc).

source_tool.$comment: R26 provenance floor on each aggregated item. NOT carried verbatim from the Compound input shape (which has no 'source_tool' field, like 'outcome') — Report DERIVES it per README 'source_tool 생산 규칙' as the upstream step that PRODUCED this aggregated sub-flow row (the sub-flow's own terminal Report step when verdict_ref is present, else the terminal step that produced the finding_refs row), propagating that provenance rather than fabricating it (and never stamping Report itself as the origin). A sub-flow with no verdict_ref/finding_refs row to derive provenance from is an input-precondition fault ⇒ result=escalate, failure_origin=upstream — enforced by the 'sub_flow_results (compound INPUT precondition)' M2 contract, since the source row lives in the INPUT shape grammar cannot see.
source_tool.description: Origin of this sub-flow result — the upstream step that PRODUCED the aggregated sub-flow row (the sub-flow's terminal Report step if verdict_ref is present, else the terminal step behind finding_refs), SYNTHESIZED/propagated by Report per README 'source_tool 생산 규칙', not an upstream-carried field and not Report itself. A sub-flow with no derivable provenance row ⇒ result=escalate, failure_origin=upstream (R26 floor — no fabricated source_tool).

sub_flow_count.$comment: NOT a CountClaim — the sub_flows array is model-assembled from the sub_flow_results input, not the stdout of a measuring command. x-m2-validator: sub_flow_count == len(sub_flows) == len(based_on.sub_flow_refs) (array-length cross-check), not a stdout re-hash.
sub_flow_count.description: Number of sub-flows aggregated. Cross-checked count == len(sub_flow_summaries.sub_flows) == len(based_on.sub_flow_refs); never a CountClaim (no command measures a model-assembled sub-flow set).

x-m2-validator-contracts.rule: P1 terminal-artifact branch — output shape matches result
x-m2-validator-contracts.check: result=synthesized ⇒ body.summary present+non-empty and the report_type per-flow minimum-bar met; result=empty_clean ⇒ empty_details present and the empty is a legitimate verdict (empty_details.evidence traces to a real Ground/Investigate fact, NOT a missing input — principle 3); result=escalate ⇒ escalate_details present and NO request_upstream_deepen emitted (principle 2).

x-m2-validator-contracts.rule: R20: re-execute verify_probe.command, compare to expected_result
x-m2-validator-contracts.check: RE-EXECUTE each finding's probe (reviewer spot-checks 3 random); mismatch = FAIL. Self-asserted 'it passes' is not truth.

x-m2-validator-contracts.rule: R36/evidence-trace: evidence must actually support the claim and cited lines must exist
x-m2-validator-contracts.check: For kind=source_manifest re-read each file, confirm sha256, assert every cited_line <= line_count and present (non-existent cited line = hallucinated citation). For kind=row_ref assert the row exists and is a Ground/Investigate output of this flow. For kind=reproduction_steps the verify_probe re-execution is the proof. Residual (evidence semantically supports the statement) = irreducible floor.

x-m2-validator-contracts.rule: R26 provenance: the named tool actually produced the finding
x-m2-validator-contracts.check: Partly M2 (re-read/re-execute the named source), partly irreducible self-asserted-truth floor (cross-verification mitigates, does not eliminate).

x-m2-validator-contracts.rule: R13 KEEP polarity: unverified must propagate, never be forbidden
x-m2-validator-contracts.check: NO 'no unverified remain' check (legacy reviewer criterion 3 / agent.md L57 DELETED per enforcement.md L41). Verify is the single gate. Whether unverified:false is honest = irreducible floor.

x-m2-validator-contracts.rule: priority re-projected from origin severity (single risk scale)
x-m2-validator-contracts.check: Each action_item priority resolves to the origin finding/risk severity (Severity scale low|med|high|critical); Report did not invent a new priority. Untraceable priority = dangling-evidence FAIL.

Each ref resolves to an existing step_runs/flow row of the expected step within this flow; not a file path. sub_flow_refs present for compound. On the escalate branch based_on is OPTIONAL and may be partial (inputs were partial/absent); whichever refs ARE present must still resolve, giving the orchestrator's NEEDS_CONTEXT routing + 5-accumulated-fail cap machine-resolvable escalate provenance.
When present, resolves to an existing queued flow/work_item row (README Activity 3 Decide.followup_flows connection).
Resolves to the Implement(prototype) result row of this Spike flow. A verdict not grounded in a real prototype row = FAIL (verdict 추측 금지).

Applies ONLY on the synthesized|empty_clean branch, which is the only branch carrying declared_next_step. declared_next_step == orchestrator-injected expected_next_step (reviewer criterion 6, CRITICAL). Mismatch = 'R16 chain violation'. No chain-outside-flow_def step claim (criterion 7). NOT APPLICABLE on result=escalate: the escalate branch carries no declared_next_step (only escalate_details); the orchestrator routes via NEEDS_CONTEXT instead of the normal successor.

No task description uses deferral language ('Spec이 finalize'/'defer to ...'); each task is concrete and directly consumable (reviewer criterion 9). Deferral = FAIL 'R19 deferral instead of concrete task'.
Two tasks sharing any target_files path MUST both have parallel_marker=false (grammar cannot see cross-task overlap).

These count model-SYNTHESIZED arrays, not a measuring command's stdout, so they are CrossCheckedCount integers, not CountClaim. Assert task_list.task_count == len(task_list.tasks); assert sub_flow_summaries.sub_flow_count == len(sub_flow_summaries.sub_flows) == len(based_on.sub_flow_refs). Any cardinality disagreement (e.g. 3 sub_flows, sub_flow_count=7, 2 sub_flow_refs) = FAIL. This is the sole guard that the three parallel compound representations (sub_flows / sub_flow_count / sub_flow_refs) agree, and that task_count matches the task array.

x-m2-validator-contracts.rule: Compound input shape well-formedness — well-formed terminal_result AND well-formed sub_flow_type AND a derivable source_tool provenance row on every aggregated sub-flow row (README 'Input preconditions' sub_flow_results row + 'Compound input shape' + 'outcome 생산 규칙' + 'source_tool 생산 규칙')
x-m2-validator-contracts.check: On report_type=compound, BEFORE synthesizing sub_flow_summaries (the input shape — Compound input shape — is not part of this OUTPUT schema, so grammar cannot see it), assert on each upstream sub_flow_results row: (1) terminal_result is well-formed ∈ {completed, abandoned, suspended}; (2) sub_flow_type is well-formed ∈ the 16 hyphenated flow-file stems {feature, bugfix, bugfix-p0, bugfix-unreproducible, refactor, performance, migration, test, chore, plan-standalone, review, release, retro, spike, exploration, compound} (cross-cutting decision 1 — closed legal set; an out-of-set/malformed sub_flow_type is otherwise silently trusted since it is an INPUT field absent from the OUTPUT schema); (3) a source_tool provenance row is DERIVABLE — i.e. the row carries verdict_ref (⇒ the sub-flow's terminal Report step) OR at least one finding_refs entry (⇒ the terminal step that produced it), so the REQUIRED output sub_flows[].source_tool can be propagated per 'source_tool 생산 규칙' rather than fabricated (R26 provenance floor — source_tool, like outcome, is absent from the input shape and is otherwise unsourced). A missing/malformed terminal_result OR an out-of-set/malformed sub_flow_type OR a sub-flow with no derivable source_tool provenance row is an input-precondition fault (not a legitimately-empty summary) ⇒ result=escalate, failure_origin=upstream (principle 3: empty-deficit not stamped as empty-legitimate; R26: no fabricated provenance). This is the only place the escalate-on-malformed-input rule is actually enforced for these fields, since the OUTPUT sub_flow_summaries carries terminal_result (required+enum) and source_tool (required) but has no sub_flow_type at all and none of them can represent the garbage-in / unsourced-provenance case (the source rows live only in the INPUT shape).

Report exists ONLY in non-code flows; a code flow producing a report (or failure_origin=report) is rejected by Verify. report_type must match the orchestrator's active flow_type; a code-flow / missing-or-malformed flow_type ⇒ result=escalate, failure_origin=upstream (NO report_type guess).
